summary: Mostly recoverable but there is a real gap: no explicit draft→final anchor remap exists, and the scaffold template's numbering convention (Exec Summary unnumbered, §1=Goals) plus the required drop of meta §16 will silently shift §17/§18 and break the five downstream docs that cite §2.5, §2–§13, §17, and Appendix A unless the remap is pinned deliberately.

findings.finding: No explicit draft→final anchor remap table exists. The draft's §1..§18 scheme does not match the binding scaffold template, and five downstream docs hard-cite architecture anchors that tasks-gen and cross-doc-invariants will resolve against. Without a pinned remap, finalization will renumber load-bearing anchors and silently break those citations.
findings.evidence: ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §1..§18 + Appendix A; scaffold/templates/ARCHITECTURE.md lines 30-107 (Exec summary UNNUMBERED, §1=Goals, Spec Anchor Index unnumbered); externally-cited arch anchors: DIAGRAM_PLAN.md lines 22,43,60,77,94,111,127,142,160 (§2,§2.5,§3-§13,§17,Appendix A), DECISIONS.md:103 (§2.5), STAKEHOLDERS.md:11 (§2.5, spec anchor index, Appendix A), RISKS.md:23 (Appendix A), CLAUDE_CODE_HANDOFF.md:96,119,123 (§2.5, Appendix A)
findings.proposed_fix: Add an explicit draft→final anchor remap to the finalization notes and HOLD these anchors stable in ARCHITECTURE.md: §2 (System overview), §2.5 (DAG & seams), §3 Shared Contracts, §4 Operational Storage, §5 Policy/Security/Egress, §6 Knowledge/Markdown/GBrain/GCL, §7 Provider & Runtime Broker, §8 Connector & Tool Gateways, §9 Temporal Workflows, §10 Local App API, §11 Electron Desktop UI, §12 Eval & Test Harness, §13 Deployment & Install, §17 Repo Scaffold, and Appendix A. Any anchor that must move requires a lockstep edit to the citing doc.

findings.finding: Draft §1 (Executive Summary) and §1A (Goals and Non-Goals) collide with the scaffold template, which leaves Executive summary UNNUMBERED and assigns §1 = Goals & non-goals. This top-of-doc remap is safe (nothing external cites §1 or §1A), but must be done deliberately so it does not cascade into renumbering §2+.
findings.evidence: ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §1 (line 11) and §1A (line 21) vs scaffold/templates/ARCHITECTURE.md line 30 (## Executive summary, unnumbered) and line 40 (## §1 — Goals & non-goals); no downstream doc references §1 or §1A (grep of docs/planning)
findings.proposed_fix: Map draft §1 Executive Summary → unnumbered 'Executive summary'; map draft §1A Goals and Non-Goals → §1 Goals & non-goals. Keep §2 and §2.5 fixed so the subsystem block §3+ does not shift.

findings.finding: Draft §16 'Architecture Gap Audit Targets for /arch-finalize' is meta/process content (instructions to the finalizer) and must be DROPPED from the binding ARCHITECTURE.md. The hazard is that dropping it opens a numbering gap and the natural reflex is to slide §17 (Repo Scaffold) and §18 (Spec Anchor Index) up into §16/§17 — which breaks DIAGRAM_PLAN sub-diagram 7's §17 citation.
findings.evidence: ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §16 lines 379-390 (checklist addressed to /arch-finalize); DIAGRAM_PLAN.md:142 spec anchors '§2.5, §17, Appendix A' for sub-diagram 7 (Parallel Build Track DAG)
findings.proposed_fix: Drop §16 entirely (do not carry it as content). Fill the §16 slot with the template's §<N> Cross-cutting concerns / §<N+1> Open questions so that §17 Repo Scaffold KEEPS its number, preserving the DIAGRAM_PLAN §17 reference without a lockstep edit.

findings.finding: Draft §17 'Repo Scaffold' is cited by DIAGRAM_PLAN sub-diagram 7, but the scaffold template has no numbered 'Repo Scaffold' section — repo-shape content tends to land in an appendix or unnumbered area. The finalizer must decide whether repo scaffold keeps a stable numbered §17 anchor or whether DIAGRAM_PLAN.md:142 is re-pointed.
findings.evidence: ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §17 lines 392-422; scaffold/templates/ARCHITECTURE.md (no Repo Scaffold heading in lines 30-107); DIAGRAM_PLAN.md:142
findings.proposed_fix: Preferred: retain a numbered §17 — Repo Scaffold section in ARCHITECTURE.md so the diagram anchor stays valid. If repo scaffold instead becomes unnumbered/appendix, update DIAGRAM_PLAN.md sub-diagram 7 spec-anchor line in the same change.

findings.finding: Draft §18 'Spec Anchor Index' is a NUMBERED section, but the scaffold template places 'Spec Anchor Index' as an UNNUMBERED section before Appendix A. Beyond renaming/renumbering, the index's right column maps REQ-* ranges to § anchors (e.g. REQ-S-001..006 → §5,§7,§8,§12); after any anchor remap this column must be regenerated, since tasks-gen and cross-doc-invariants resolve requirements-to-section through this table.
findings.evidence: ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §18 lines 424-437 (numbered table); scaffold/templates/ARCHITECTURE.md line 89 (## Spec Anchor Index, unnumbered); STAKEHOLDERS.md:11 names 'spec anchor index' as the tasks-gen contract
findings.proposed_fix: Convert §18 to the template's unnumbered 'Spec Anchor Index' and regenerate every § value in the right column against the final anchor numbers after the remap. Verify each REQ-ID range still resolves to an existing section heading.

findings.finding: REQUIREMENTS.md's many §-references (e.g. §9.1, §11.1, §17, §20.1) are PRD source anchors, not architecture anchors, so they are NOT affected by architecture renumbering. Worth stating explicitly in the finalization notes so a reviewer does not mistakenly try to 'fix' them when the architecture sections move, and so the inverse mapping (§18 index → REQ-IDs) only depends on the stable REQ-ID column in REQUIREMENTS.md.
findings.evidence: REQUIREMENTS.md Source column lines 9-90 cite PRD sections (§9.1, §11.1, §17, §19.1, §20.1) which exist only in system_of_work_assistant_prd_v0_3.md, not in ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md
findings.proposed_fix: In the Spec Anchor Index note, state that REQUIREMENTS.md §-refs are PRD anchors (stable, untouched) and only the architecture §-column of the index is regenerated on remap.

findings.finding: §2.5 is already aligned (present verbatim in both draft and template, line 50) and is the single most-cited cross-doc anchor (DIAGRAM_PLAN, DECISIONS, STAKEHOLDERS, CLAUDE_CODE_HANDOFF). It must be preserved as the exact literal '§2.5' (sub-decimal anchor) and not normalized to a plain integer during finalization.
findings.evidence: ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §2.5 line 74; scaffold/templates/ARCHITECTURE.md line 50; cited at DIAGRAM_PLAN.md:142, DECISIONS.md:103, STAKEHOLDERS.md:11, CLAUDE_CODE_HANDOFF.md:96,119
findings.proposed_fix: Keep the literal '§2.5 — Subsystem dependency DAG & parallelization seams' heading string unchanged so anchor links resolve.
